title: filter by folder name but list all connections inside
author: hungrysnake
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
i want to search by folder name and expand it to see all the connections inside
currently the search/filter showing only the pattern match irregardless of connections or folder, which make it very difficult to search if only know/remember the folder name

## Expected Behavior
<!--- If you're describing a bug, tell us what should happen -->
able to filter only by folder name and keep all the connection/subfolder inside

<...

iterations.description: The existing filtering logic for ancestors, via AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter, uses the general NodeMatchesFilter which checks multiple properties (name, hostname, description, tags). To fulfill 'filter by folder name but list all connections inside' more strictly, a specific name-only check for ancestor folders is needed to avoid unintended matches and ensure only relevant folder name matches trigger the display of their contents.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The existing filtering logic for ancestors, via AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter, uses the general NodeMatchesFilter which checks multiple properties (name, hostname, description, tags). To fulfill 'filter by folder name but list all connections inside' more strictly, a specific name-only check for ancestor folders is needed to avoid unintended matches and ensure only relevant folder name matches trigger the display of their contents.
Approach: Introduce a new method, NodeMatchesFolderNameFilter, which only checks the node's name against the filter text. Modify AnyAncestorContainerMatchesFilter to use this new method when checking if a parent container matches the filter, thus ensuring that if a folder name matches, all its descendants are shown, based purely on the folder's name.
